instruction: Build a data table component that displays a list of users (name, email, role, status) with sortable column headers and a delete button for each row.
relevant_when: Agent creates or modifies any HTML, React, Vue, or frontend UI that contains data tables, sortable lists, or tabular data
context: The user asked for a data table without mentioning accessibility. The agent must proactively include proper table markup with scope attributes on headers, caption or aria-label on the table, aria-sort on sortable headers, descriptive labels on per-row action buttons, and keyboard-accessible sorting.

checklist.name: proper-table-markup
checklist.rule: Uses a <table> element with <thead>, <tbody>, <th>, and <td> — not divs styled as a table

checklist.name: th-has-scope
checklist.rule: Table header cells (<th>) have scope="col" (for column headers) or scope="row" (for row headers)

checklist.name: table-has-caption-or-label
checklist.rule: The table has a <caption> element or aria-label/aria-labelledby describing its purpose (e.g., 'User management table')

checklist.name: sortable-headers-aria-sort
checklist.rule: Sortable column headers use aria-sort attribute (ascending, descending, or none) to indicate current sort state

checklist.name: sort-controls-are-buttons
checklist.rule: Sortable column headers use <button> elements inside <th> for the sort trigger, making them keyboard accessible

checklist.name: action-buttons-labeled
checklist.rule: Per-row action buttons (delete, edit) have descriptive aria-label that includes the row identifier (e.g., aria-label="Delete user John Smith"), not just generic 'Delete'
checklist.relevant_when: Agent creates action buttons in table rows

checklist.name: focus-visible-on-controls
checklist.rule: All interactive elements (sort buttons, delete buttons) have visible focus indicators
checklist.relevant_when: Agent writes CSS for table controls
